observer-intuition.html.title: Observer Intuition
observer-intuition.html.description: See State FeedbackThe separation principle still holds when using an augmented-state observer that estimates disturbances.For a plantwith a constant disturbance modeldefine the augmented stateAn observer can estimate both plant states and disturbance:The closed-loop poles are the union of: Controller poles of the augmented system Observer poles of the augmented system The controller and observer may therefore still be designed independently.A key realization:The observer's job is estimation, not compensation.The observer estimatesbut does not apply any control action.Compensation occurs only when the controller uses that estimate:For example,The most important insight from the discussion:The observer is not trying to driveorInstead it is trying to driveto zero.The observer dynamics are fundamentally estimation-error dynamics.A perfect observer converges towhich is generally nonzero.SupposeandSubstituting:The plant no longer sees the full disturbance.It sees only the residual disturbanceIfthe disturbance disappears from the plant dynamics.This does not mean the observer has lost track of the disturbance.It means the disturbance has been perfectly compensated.Integral control introducesA disturbance observer introducesBoth are mechanisms for learning the steady-state bias required to reject a constant disturbance.Integral action learns it indirectly through accumulated tracking error.Disturbance estimation learns it directly through model mismatch.Both ultimately generate the control bias needed to satisfyBoth states can contribute a steady-state control bias:This can create the impression that there must be hidden dynamics.The important distinction: Similar purpose does not imply identical dynamics. Similar purpose does not imply zero dynamics. The integrator and disturbance estimate are generally driven by different signals:vs.Therefore they usually evolve differently.Only special choices of gains and structure would make them dynamically equivalent.The redundancy intuition is closer to pole-zero cancellation than to classical zero dynamics.If two controller states learn the same bias term and evolve identically, one state direction can become effectively invisible to the plant.This resembles: Non-minimal realizations Pole-zero cancellation Hidden internal modes However, this only occurs under special circumstances.In general, the states remain dynamically distinct.Plant states:Plant order = 2Observer states:Observer order = 3Total without integrator:Adding integral control:Using disturbance feedback instead of integral action:Thus disturbance feedback can reduce controller order by one state in this implementation.A major practical insight:The disturbance estimate is generated from observer innovation:If measurements are quantized, thencontains: True disturbances Model mismatch Quantization artifacts Thereforemay be highly active and noisy.Using disturbance feedback creates a direct path:Measurement noise → Observer → Disturbance estimate → Control inputwhich can inject noise into the actuator.The integrator evolves according toAn integrator naturally accumulates persistent bias and tends to average out high-frequency quantization effects.Therefore: Disturbance feedback may react to quantization artifacts. Integral action primarily reacts to long-term offset. In systems where the disturbance state is being used by the observer to absorb measurement quantization or model mismatch, integral action may produce cleaner control behavior despite appearing theoretically redundant.A disturbance observer and an integrator both provide mechanisms for rejecting constant disturbances.The disturbance observer: Learns the disturbance explicitly. Can provide faster compensation. Can reduce controller order if an augmented observer already exists. May reintroduce measurement noise through the disturbance feedback path. Integral action: Learns the required compensation indirectly through accumulated error. Is highly robust to modeling errors. Naturally suppresses high-frequency measurement artifacts. Often produces cleaner behavior when disturbance estimates are noisy. For systems with significant quantization or observer-induced disturbance-estimate noise, integral action may be preferable even when a disturbance estimate is available.

headers.heading: Disturbance Observers vs Integral Action — Key Insights
1. Separation Principle with Augmented Disturbance States
2. The Observer Does Not Compensate Disturbances
3. The Important Quantity Is Estimation Error
4. Why Disturbance Compensation Does Not Hide the Disturbance
5. Integral Action and Disturbance Estimation Solve Similar Problems
6. Why Integral Action and Disturbance Feedback Can Feel Redundant
8. State Counting for the Motor Example
9. Why Integral Action May Still Be Better
10. Integral Action Naturally Filters Quantization Effects

info_showCustomIcons.description: Show custom icons for files and folders

info_showDefaultFolderIcons.description: Show a default icon of a folder for every folder in the tree

info_showDefaultFileIcons.description: Show a default icon of a file for every file in the tree

info_defaultFolderIcon.description: The icon to use for folders. Prefix with 'lucide//' to use a Lucide icon

info_defaultFileIcon.description: The icon to use for files. Prefix with 'lucide//' to use a Lucide icon

info_defaultMediaIcon.description: The icon to use for media files. Prefix with 'lucide//' to use a Lucide icon

info_exposeStartingPath.description: Whether or not to show the current file in the file tree when the page is first loaded

info_showOrphanNodes.description: Show nodes that are not connected to any other nodes.

info_showAttachments.description: Show attachments like images and PDFs as nodes in the graph.

info_allowGlobalGraph.description: Allow the user to view the global graph of all nodes.

info_allowExpand.description: Allow the user to pop-out the graph view to take up the whole screen

info_attractionForce.description: How much should linked nodes attract each other? This will make the graph appear more clustered.

info_linkLength.description: How long should the links between nodes be? The shorter the links the more connected nodes will cluster together.

info_repulsionForce.description: How much should nodes repel each other? This will make disconnected parts more spread out.

info_centralForce.description: How much should nodes be attracted to the center? This will make the graph appear more dense and circular.

info_edgePruning.description: Edges with a length above this threshold will not be rendered, however they will still contribute to the simulation. This can help large tangled graphs look more organised. Hovering over a node will still display these links.

info_minNodeRadius.description: How small should the smallest nodes be? The smaller a node is the less it will attract other nodes.

info_maxNodeRadius.description: How large should the largest nodes be? Nodes are sized by how many links they have. The larger a node is the more it will attract other nodes. This can be used to create a good grouping around the most important nodes.

info_allowResizing.description: Whether or not to allow the sidebars to be resized

info_allowCollapsing.description: Whether or not to allow the sidebars to be collapsed

info_rightDefaultWidth.description: The default width of the right sidebar

info_leftDefaultWidth.description: The default width of the left sidebar

info_selector.description: CSS selector for an element. The feature will be placed relative to this element.

info_type.description: Will this feature be placed before, after, or inside (at the beggining or end).

info_featurePlacement.description: Where to place this feature on the page. (Relative to the selector)

info_sourcePath.description: The local path to the source .html file which will be included.

info_allowFoldingLists.description: Whether or not to allow lists to be folded

info_allowFoldingHeadings.description: Whether or not to allow headings to be folded

info_siteUrl.description: The url that this site will be hosted at

info_authorName.description: The name of the author of the site
